title: dalbergia riparia
intro: Dalbergia riparia, also known as the Indian rosewood or East Indian rosewood, is a species of tree in the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Dalbergia riparia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions, as well as its potential to prevent the growth of certain types of cancer cells. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or oil, which are used to treat a range of ailments, from respiratory issues to gastrointestinal problems.
